2603.18761.title: NeuroGame Transformer: Gibbs-Inspired Attention Driven by Game Theory and Statistical Physics
2603.18761.paper_summary: Standard attention mechanisms in transformers are limited by their pairwise formulation, which hinders the modeling of higher-order dependencies among tokens. We introduce the NeuroGame Transformer (NGT) to overcome this by reconceptualizing attention through a dual perspective: tokens are treated simultaneously as players in a cooperative game and as interacting spins in a statistical physics system. Token importance is quantified using two complementary game-theoretic concepts -- Shapley values for global, permutation-based attribution and Banzhaf indices for local, coalition-level influence. These are combined via a learnable gating parameter to form an external magnetic field, while pairwise interaction potentials capture synergistic relationships. The system's energy follows an Ising Hamiltonian, with attention weights emerging as marginal probabilities under the Gibbs distribution, efficiently computed via mean-field equations. To ensure scalability despite the exponential coalition space, we develop importance-weighted Monte Carlo estimators with Gibbs-distributed weights. This approach avoids explicit exponential factors, ensuring numerical stability for long sequences. We provide theoretical convergence guarantees and characterize the fairness-sensitivity trade-off governed by the interpolation parameter. Experimental results demonstrate that the NeuroGame Transformer achieves strong performance across SNLI, and MNLI-matched, outperforming some major efficient transformer baselines. On SNLI, it attains a test accuracy of 86.4\% (with a peak validation accuracy of 86.6\%), surpassing ALBERT-Base and remaining highly competitive with RoBERTa-Base. Code is available at https://github.com/dbouchaffra/NeuroGame-Transformer.
2603.18761.paper_summary_zh: 標準的注意力機制在Transformer中受到其成對公式的限制，這妨礙了對標記之間更高階依賴關係的建模。<lb/>我們引入了神經遊戲Transformer（NeuroGame Transformer, NGT）來克服這一點，通過雙重視角重新概念化注意力：標記同時被視為合作遊戲中的玩家和統計物理系統中的相互作用自旋。<lb/>標記的重要性使用兩個互補的博弈論概念來量化——Shapley值用於全球的基於排列的歸因，Banzhaf指數用於局部的聯盟級影響。<lb/>這些通過可學習的閘參數結合，形成一個外部磁場，而成對互動潛能則捕捉協同關係。<lb/>系統的能量遵循伊辛哈密頓量，注意力權重作為吉布斯分佈下的邊際概率出現，並通過均場方程高效計算。<lb/>為了確保儘管存在指數聯盟空間仍能擴展，我們開發了帶有吉布斯分佈權重的重要性加權蒙特卡羅估計器。<lb/>這種方法避免了顯式的指數因子，確保了長序列的數值穩定性。<lb/>我們提供了理論收斂保證，並描述了由插值參數主導的公平性與敏感性之間的權衡。<lb/>實驗結果顯示，神經遊戲Transformer在SNLI和MNLI匹配上表現強勁，超越了一些主要的高效Transformer基準。<lb/>在SNLI上，它達到了86.4%的測試準確率（峰值驗證準確率為86.6%），超越了ALBERT-Base，並與RoBERTa-Base保持高度競爭。<lb/>代碼可在https://github.com/dbouchaffra/NeuroGame-Transformer獲得。
2603.18761.author: Djamel Bouchaffra et.al.
2603.18761.authors: Djamel Bouchaffra, Fayçal Ykhlef, Hanene Azzag, Mustapha Lebbah, Bilal Faye
